  "confidence": 1.0,

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Technician began removing the inverter cover without performing a meter verification of absence of voltage.",
    "The Class 0 high-voltage gloves in use have an expired test date (expired nine months ago).",
    "The service disconnect was removed but the system was not verified as safe/de-energized before proceeding with disassembly."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately.",
    "Replace the expired Class 0 gloves with a pair that has a current, valid certified test date (tested within the last 6 months).",
    "Perform a visual and physical inflation test on the new gloves before use.",
    "Use a properly rated Category III or IV digital multimeter to perform a three-point test (live-dead-live) to verify the absence of voltage at the inverter terminals before touching any internal components."
  ],
  "rationale": "The technician is attempting to access high-voltage components (inside the inverter) without verifying that the system is actually de-energized. Additionally, the personal protective equipment (PPE) being used is expired and cannot be trusted to provide the necessary dielectric protection, creating an immediate, critical risk of lethal electrical shock.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.137 (Electrical Protective Equipment)",
    "NFPA 70E (Standard for Electrical Safety in the Workplace)",
    "OEM High-Voltage De-energization and Zero-Voltage Verification Procedures"
  ]
}
